SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.store.jdbc.serializer.cipher

Interface PayloadCipher

All Known Implementing Classes:
Base64PayloadCipher
public interface PayloadCipher
Strategy for encrypting and decrypting the serialized payload string before it is written to / after it is read from the JDBC failover store. Applies only to the JDBC store — other stores (in-memory, Caffeine) keep live objects and never persist a payload string.

Envelope and dispatch

Implementations deal in raw ciphertext only; they do not add or parse the ENC(...) envelope. The EncryptingSerializer owns the envelope, writing each value as ENC(<id>:<ciphertext>) and, on read, dispatching to the registered cipher whose id() matches the tag. This lets a single store hold rows written by different ciphers (e.g. ENC(b64:...) and ENC(aesgcm:...)) and still decrypt each correctly — the basis for key/algorithm rotation.

Contract

  • id() must be short, stable and unique across all registered ciphers; it is persisted inside every encrypted row, so changing it strands existing rows.
  • encrypt(java.lang.String)/decrypt(java.lang.String) must round-trip: decrypt(encrypt(x)).equals(x).
  • A null input returns null.
  • decrypt must throw (not return garbage) when the input was not produced by this cipher, so a misconfiguration surfaces loudly rather than corrupting the payload.

Declare a PayloadCipher bean to provide real encryption (AES-GCM, a KMS/Jasypt-backed implementation, …). The built-in Base64PayloadCipher is encoding only — not security.

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:
  • EncryptingSerializer
  • Base64PayloadCipher

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @Nullable String
    decrypt(@Nullable String ciphertext)
    Decrypts raw ciphertext (no envelope) back to the serialized payload string.
    @Nullable String
    encrypt(@Nullable String plaintext)
    Encrypts a serialized payload string to raw ciphertext (no envelope).
    String
    id()
    Short, stable, unique identifier persisted in the ENC(<id>:...) envelope and used on read to select this cipher.

  • Method Details

    • id

      String id()
      Short, stable, unique identifier persisted in the ENC(<id>:...) envelope and used on read to select this cipher. Example: "b64", "aesgcm".
      Returns:
      the cipher id; must not be null, empty, or contain ':' or ')'

    • encrypt

      @Nullable String encrypt(@Nullable String plaintext)
      Encrypts a serialized payload string to raw ciphertext (no envelope).
      Parameters:
      plaintext - the serialized payload; null is allowed
      Returns:
      the raw ciphertext, or null if plaintext is null

    • decrypt

      @Nullable String decrypt(@Nullable String ciphertext)
      Decrypts raw ciphertext (no envelope) back to the serialized payload string.
      Parameters:
      ciphertext - the raw ciphertext previously returned by encrypt(java.lang.String); null is allowed
      Returns:
      the serialized payload, or null if ciphertext is null
